message:
sync: Close bd-3je after E2E quality tests verified passing

E2E tests demonstrate correct behavior:
- Pass/fail/skip behaviors correct
- Durations recorded in JSON output
- Schema validation passes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-39w","title":"E2E: dsr doctor (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr doctor (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify doctor checks real dependencies and produces actionable output.

## Approach
- Run doctor in a clean env and capture missing deps.
- Run with `--json` to validate schema and stream separation.

## Logging
- Log detected deps and their status.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing deps are reported clearly.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.
- [ ] Non-interactive mode does not prompt.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:15.972960698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:15:21.027262370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:15:21.027243895Z","close_reason":"E2E doctor tests already complete in test_doctor.sh: 15 tests covering help, JSON validation, dependency detection, quick mode, fix mode, and exit codes. All passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39w","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:50.126884944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-39w","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:52.163395945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cn","title":"Unit tests for github.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for github.sh Module

## Module Overview
github.sh provides GitHub API access with caching and rate limiting:
- `gh_check` - Verify gh CLI + authentication
- `gh_check_token` - Validate GITHUB_TOKEN
- `gh_api` - Make API requests with caching
- `gh_workflow_runs` - List workflow runs
- `gh_releases` - List releases
- `gh_create_release` - Create a release
- `gh_upload_asset` - Upload release asset

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Authentication Tests
- `gh_check` succeeds when gh is authenticated
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when gh not installed
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when not authenticated
- `gh_check_token` validates GITHUB_TOKEN format
- `gh_check_token` detects expired/invalid tokens (via API call)

### 2. Caching Tests
- Cache directory created under XDG_CACHE_HOME
- First request creates cache entry
- Second request within TTL uses cache
- Request after TTL expiry fetches fresh
- ETag-based validation (304 Not Modified)
- Cache key includes endpoint parameters

### 3. Rate Limiting Tests
- Rate limit detection via response headers
- Retry with backoff on 429 response
- Maximum retry count enforced (3)
- Rate limit warning logged

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Network errors produce clear messages
- 404 responses handled correctly
- 401 (auth) errors trigger re-auth hint
- 500 errors trigger retry logic

### 5. JSON Output Tests
- `--json` mode returns valid JSON
- Error responses follow envelope schema
- Success responses include metadata

## Test Approach
- Test validation/formatting logic WITHOUT live API calls
- Use skip markers for tests requiring network
- Cache tests use temp directory
- Rate limit tests use mock time if needed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:14:18.130245753Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:14:18.129628029Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cn","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ib","title":"E2E: dsr help/version smoke tests","description":"# E2E: dsr help/version smoke tests

## Purpose
Ensure CLI help/version output works across commands and respects --json.

## Scope
- `dsr --help`, `dsr <cmd> --help`
- `dsr --version` with and without --json

## Logging
- Capture stdout/stderr to verify stream separation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help text exits 0.
- [ ] Version JSON is valid and on stdout only.
- [ ] No unexpected stderr output on success.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:57:09.741541708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:11.423957059Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ib","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:09.839471386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ib","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:11.423248314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Purpose
Exercise quality gate execution using real commands (true/false/sleep) and real repos.d config.

## Approach
- Create temp repos config with simple check commands.
- Run `dsr quality <tool>` and verify results and exit codes.
- Validate --json output schema and stderr separation.

## Logging
- Log the check commands executed and timing.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass/fail/skip behaviors are correct.
- [ ] Durations recorded in output.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","notes":"Added scripts/tests/test_quality_e2e.sh covering dsr quality pass/fail/skip with real commands, JSON validation, and stream separation. Shellcheck clean.  {"id":"bd-3kr","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management

Centralize dsr configuration. Config at ~/.config/dsr/ including config.yaml, repos.yaml, hosts.yaml. Commands: dsr config show/edit/validate/init. Environment overrides via DSR_* vars.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Config loaded at startup
- [ ] Environment overrides work
- [ ] Validation catches errors","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:22:57.697466261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:06.737659325Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:06.737652281Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3lo","title":"EPIC: Real-Behavior Test Suite (No Mocks)","description":"# EPIC: Real-Behavior Test Suite (No Mocks)

## Purpose
Establish full unit + E2E coverage using real behavior (no mocks/fakes), with detailed logging that makes failures immediately diagnosable.

## Non-Negotiables
- **No mocks/fakes** in unit or E2E tests.
- Use **real behavior** and real binaries where possible.
- If external deps are missing (gh auth, minisign, docker, act), tests must **skip with explicit log** explaining what to install/configure.
- **Stdout is data**, stderr is human logs; test harness must capture both and print on failure.

## Success Criteria
- All src/ modules have unit tests that exercise real code paths.
- Every CLI command has an E2E script with structured logging.
- Skips are explicit and actionable (never silent).
- JSON output + stream separation validated for all  {"id":"bd-3op","title":"Unit tests for host_selector.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for host_selector.sh Module

## Module Overview
host_selector.sh provides host selection and concurrency management:
- `select_host` - Choose optimal host for target platform
- `host_queue_enqueue` - Add build to host queue
- `host_queue_dequeue` - Get next build from queue
- `host_concurrency_check` - Verify host under limit
- `host_capability_match` - Match target to host capabilities

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Host Selection Tests
- Select Linux host for linux/amd64 target
- Select macOS host for darwin/arm64 target
- Select Windows host for windows/amd64 target
- Fallback when preferred host unhealthy
- Error when no capable host available

### 2. Capability Matching Tests
- Host with `capabilities: [rust, go]` matches Go project
- Host without required capability rejected
- Multiple capable hosts returns highest priority

### 3. Concurrency Tests
- Respect per-host concurrency limit
- Queue builds when at capacity
- Dequeue in FIFO order
- Track running builds per host

### 4. Health Integration Tests
- Skip unhealthy hosts in selection
- Re-evaluate after health change
- Log selection rationale

## Test Approach
- Use temp directory for state files
- Mock host health status
- Test deterministic selection (same inputs → same output)
- Verify JSON output format","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T21:49:55.754148138Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T21:49:55.754097062Z","close_reason":"Fixed yq stub to use 'concurrency' field, fixed test isolation, fixed jq precedence in queue status test. All 11 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3op","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
